subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Why Accidents Aren't Accidents
description: Learn Why Accidents Aren't Accidents in one minute.

hook.text: Safety scientists stopped using the word 'accident' because it implies nobody's at fault.

buildup.text: Most workplace injuries result from predictable chains of failures, not random bad luck.

discovery.text: Heinrich's pyramid: for every serious injury, there are 29 minor ones and 300 near misses.

twist.text: Investigating near misses prevents the serious injury at the top of the pyramid.

climax.text: Modern safety science replaces 'accident' with 'incident'—implying preventable, not inevitable.

punchline.text: If it was predictable, it was preventable. That changes everything.

quiz.question: According to Heinrich's pyramid, how many near misses occur for every serious injury?
